example: Hey Mike, how's it going?

pattern: What have you been up to?
example: What have you been up to lately?
pron: Vat hev yu bin ap tu?

example: Oh wow, long time no see! How are you?
I have to go, see you later.
Bye! Take care!
Here is my number, let's keep in touch.
Say hi to your mom for me.
Hi, I'm Sarah. Nice to meet you.
exampleTr: Merhaba, ben Sarah. Tanıştığımıza memnun oldum.

example: Hey! What's new with you?
I gotta run, catch you later!
I heard the news. How are you holding up?

meaning: Yıllar oldu! / Çok uzun zaman oldu!
example: Wow, it's been ages since we last met!
exampleTr: Vay, son görüşmemizden beri çok uzun zaman oldu!

example: See you tomorrow, have a good one!

meaning: Neler oluyor? / Ne var ne yok?
example: Hey, what's going on?
It's late, I'd better get going.
exampleTr: Geç oldu, artık gitsem iyi olacak.

example: You're always welcome, drop by anytime.
exampleTr: Her zaman hoş gelirsin, istediğin zaman uğra.

example: Don't get up, I'll see myself out.
Hey John! Good to see you again.
exampleTr: Hey John! Seni tekrar görmek güzel.

example: Sorry, I'm in a rush right now.
exampleTr: Özür dilerim, şu an acelem var.

example: No pressure, take your time.
I'm not sure about the answer.
To be honest, I didn't like it.
It might work, it's worth a try.
Never mind, I found my keys.
How is the project? So far so good.
exampleTr: Proje nasıl? Şimdilik her şey yolunda.

example: Let's meet at 5 PM. Sounds good!
Pizza or pasta? It's up to you.
exampleTr: Pizza mı makarna mı? Sana kalmış.

example: Long story short, we missed the bus.
I will check and get back to you.
I'm on my way, see you in 5 minutes.
You forgot? No worries, it happens.
exampleTr: Unuttun mu? Sorun değil, olur böyle şeyler.

example: Oh, that makes sense now.
Where is the remote? I have no idea.
Are you coming? It depends on the weather.
I know it's late but I can't help it.
exampleTr: Geç olduğunu biliyorum ama elimde değil.

example: What's the point of arguing?
By the way, did you call your mom?
I'm sick of this traffic.
That's a big decision, let me think about it.
exampleTr: Bu büyük bir karar, bir düşüneyim.

example: It's complicated, you know what I mean?
exampleTr: Karmaşık, ne demek istediğimi anlıyor musun?

example: Relax, I'm just kidding!

meaning: Mesele de o / İşte sorun o.
example: That's the thing, I don't have the money.
I'm telling you, this place is amazing.
Here's the thing, I already said yes.
exampleTr: Mesele şu ki, ben zaten evet dedim.

example: Guess what! I got the job!
exampleTr: Bil bakalım ne oldu! İşi aldım!

example: As far as I know, the meeting is at 3.
For what it's worth, I think you're right.
exampleTr: Ne kadar değeri varsa, bence haklısın.

example: I don't want to go out. Fair enough.
Did you like the movie? Not really.
I'm so tired. Same here.
You have a point, let's reconsider.
Tea or coffee? I don't mind.
exampleTr: Çay mı kahve mi? Fark etmez.

example: The exam is cancelled? What a relief!
exampleTr: Sınav iptal mi? Ne büyük rahatlama!

example: The food was delicious. I bet!
Work has been stressful. Tell me about it.
exampleTr: İş stresli oldu. Bana mı anlatıyorsun.

meaning: Seni anlıyorum / Ben de aynısını yaşadım.
example: Mondays are tough. I can relate.

meaning: Olan oldu / Yapacak bir şey yok.
example: We lost the game, it is what it is.
exampleTr: Maçı kaybettik, yapacak bir şey yok.

example: We don't have much time, let's get to the point.
That name rings a bell.
The trip is next week, I'm looking forward to it!
exampleTr: Gezi gelecek hafta, dört gözle bekliyorum!

example: I'm fed up with this noise.
I can't stand the heat.
The exam is over, I'm so relieved.
I didn't mean to hurt you.
You passed! I'm proud of you.
What they think? I couldn't care less.
The movie was amazing, it blew my mind.
I'm having second thoughts about the job offer.
You spilled coffee? It's not a big deal.
exampleTr: Kahve mi döktün? Büyük mesele değil.

example: I'm grateful for your support.
The test was negative? That's a relief.
exampleTr: Test negatif mi? Oh be, rahatladım.

example: I feel like staying home today.
I'm on the fence about buying a new car.
That's unfair. I totally agree.
I beg to differ, I think it's a bad idea.
exampleTr: Farklı düşünüyorum, bence kötü bir fikir.

example: I have so much work, I'm overwhelmed.
exampleTr: Çok fazla işim var, bunalmış hissediyorum.

example: Seeing you smile makes me happy.
exampleTr: Senin gülümsediğini görmek beni mutlu ediyor.

example: I'm not in the mood for going out.
That concert was amazing, I can't get over it.
I'm sick and tired of waiting.
That noise gets on my nerves.
Thank you for the gift, I'm so touched.
I was blown away by the performance.
I got the promotion, I'm thrilled!
I'm torn between the two options.

pattern: It means a lot to me.
example: Your support means a lot to me.
pron: İt minz e lat tu mi.

example: I got married, I couldn't be happier!
Can you give me a hand with this?

pattern: Could you do me a favor?
example: Could you do me a favor and close the window?
exampleTr: Bir iyilik yapıp pencereyi kapatır mısın?
pron: Kud yu du mi e feyvır?

example: Would you mind turning down the music?
exampleTr: Müziği kısar mısın, sakıncası olur mu?

example: If you could help, I'd appreciate it.
Thanks for covering for me, I owe you one.
exampleTr: Yerime geçtiğin için teşekkürler, sana borçluyum.

example: I'll handle it, don't bother.
Can I sit here? Sure, go ahead.
There's food on the table, help yourself.
exampleTr: Masada yemek var, buyur kendin al.

example: Sorry, I didn't catch that. Can you repeat?
Bear with me, I'm almost done.
Do you have a minute? I need to talk.
exampleTr: Bir dakikan var mı? Konuşmam lazım.

pattern: Can you keep an eye on...?
example: Can you keep an eye on my bag?
pron: Ken yu kip en ay on...?

example: Hey, I need a favor. Can you pick me up?
exampleTr: Hey, bir iyiliğe ihtiyacım var. Beni alabilir misin?

pattern: Could you hold this for me?
example: Could you hold this for me for a second?
pron: Kud yu hold dis for mi?

example: Sorry to bother you, but can I ask something?
exampleTr: Rahatsız ettiğim için özür dilerim, bir şey sorabilir miyim?

example: I could use some help with the boxes.

pattern: Would you be so kind as to...?
example: Would you be so kind as to open the door?
pron: Vud yu bi so kaynd ez tu...?

example: I was wondering if you could help me.
Can you spare a minute? I need advice.
exampleTr: Bir dakika ayırabilir misin? Tavsiye lazım.

example: Excuse me, how much is this shirt?
I don't have cash, can I pay by card?
I'd like to order a coffee, please.
exampleTr: Bir kahve sipariş etmek istiyorum, lütfen.

example: We're done, can I get the check please?
exampleTr: Biz bittik, hesabı alabilir miyim lütfen?

example: Is there a discount for students?
Can I help you? No thanks, I'm just browsing.
exampleTr: Yardımcı olabilir miyim? Hayır teşekkürler, sadece bakıyorum.

pattern: Do you have this in a different size?
example: I like this dress. Do you have it in a smaller size?
exampleTr: Bu elbiseyi beğendim. Daha küçük bedeni var mı?
pron: Du yu hev dis in e difırınt sayz?

example: Can I try this jacket on?
It fits perfectly, I'll take it.
What do you recommend from the menu?
Hi, could I have the menu please?
Is this jacket on sale?
Here you go, keep the change.
That looks delicious, I'll have what she's having.

pattern: Could we get a table for two?
example: Good evening, could we get a table for two?
exampleTr: İyi akşamlar, iki kişilik masa alabilir miyiz?
pron: Kud vi get e teybıl for tu?

example: I'm allergic to nuts, is this safe?
exampleTr: Fıstığa alerjim var, bu güvenli mi?

pattern: Can I see the wine list?
example: Can I see the wine list, please?
pron: Ken ay si dı vayn list?

pattern: This is not what I ordered.
example: Excuse me, this is not what I ordered.
pron: Dis iz nat vat ay ordırd.

example: This doesn't work, can I get a refund?
Do you take cash or only cards?
exampleTr: Nakit mi kabul ediyorsunuz yoksa sadece kart mı?

example: Where's the fitting room, please?
I'd like a receipt for this purchase, please.
exampleTr: Bu alışveriş için fiş istiyorum lütfen.

example: Is the tip included in the bill?

pattern: Can I get this to go?
example: Can I get this coffee to go?
pron: Ken ay get dis tu go?

example: Excuse me, how do I get to the train station?
Is the airport far from here?
Turn right at the traffic light.
Go straight ahead for two blocks.
Excuse me, I'm lost. Can you help me?
Where is the nearest pharmacy?
Which platform does the train leave from?
How long does it take to get there by bus?
exampleTr: Otobüsle oraya gitmek ne kadar sürer?

pattern: Can you drop me off at...?
example: Can you drop me off at the next corner?
exampleTr: Beni bir sonraki köşede indirir misin?
pron: Ken yu drop mi of et...?

example: Oh no, I missed my stop!

pattern: Is this the right way to...?
example: Excuse me, is this the right way to the museum?
exampleTr: Pardon, müzeye giden yol bu mu?
pron: İz dis dı rayt vey tu...?

example: The bank? It's just around the corner.
It's the big red building, you can't miss it.

pattern: I need to catch the next bus.
example: Hurry, I need to catch the next bus!
exampleTr: Acele et, bir sonraki otobüsü yakalamam lazım!
pron: Ay nid tu keç dı nekst bas.

example: Does this bus go to the city center?
exampleTr: Bu otobüs şehir merkezine gidiyor mu?

pattern: Where can I get a taxi?
example: Excuse me, where can I get a taxi?
pron: Ver ken ay get e teksi?

example: Is there a shortcut to the station?
Sorry, I'm running late for the meeting.
Enough small talk, let's get down to business.
We're still waiting, I'll keep you posted.
Something came up, can we reschedule?
Can't join lunch today, I'm swamped.
exampleTr: Bugün öğle yemeğine gelemem, işim başımdan aşkın.

pattern: Let me get back to you on that.
example: Good question, let me get back to you on that.
exampleTr: İyi soru, o konuda sana dönerim.
pron: Let mi get bek tu yu on det.

example: Good, it seems we're on the same page.
Sorry, the deadline slipped my mind.
exampleTr: Özür dilerim, son tarih aklımdan çıkmış.

example: Don't worry about the report, I'll handle it.
What's the deadline for this project?
exampleTr: Bu projenin son tarihi ne zaman?

example: It's getting late, let's wrap it up.
Don't stress, I'll take care of it.
I'm busy now, let's touch base later.
Good idea, I'll follow up on that.
We need ideas, let's brainstorm.
exampleTr: Fikirlere ihtiyacımız var, beyin fırtınası yapalım.

example: Is the report ready? I'm working on it.
I missed the meeting, can you fill me in?
That decision? That's above my pay grade.
exampleTr: O karar mı? Bu benim yetki alanımın üstünde.

example: Good point, let's circle back to that later.
Free on Thursday? I'll pencil you in.
exampleTr: Perşembe müsait misin? Seni programa yazayım.

pattern: We need to step up our game.
example: The competitors are strong, we need to step up our game.
pron: Vi nid tu step ap avr geym.

pattern: Let's put a pin in that.
example: Interesting idea, let's put a pin in that for now.
pron: Lets put e pin in det.

example: I'm in a meeting, can I call you back?
Hello? You're breaking up, I can't hear you.
I'll text you the address.

pattern: Let me put you on speaker.
example: Hold on, let me put you on speaker.
pron: Let mi put yu on spikır.

example: I called earlier and left you a message.
exampleTr: Daha önce aradım ve sana mesaj bıraktım.

example: Sorry, wrong number. Have a good day.
It's noisy here, can you speak up?
exampleTr: Burası gürültülü, daha yüksek sesle konuşabilir misin?

example: When I arrive, I'll drop you a line.
Hang on a second, I'll check.
I'll give you a ring when I'm free.
Who's calling, please? Let me transfer you.
I tried calling but the line is busy.

pattern: I'll get back to you ASAP.
example: Let me check and I'll get back to you ASAP.
exampleTr: Kontrol edeyim, en kısa sürede dönerim.
pron: Ayl get bek tu yu ey-es-ey-pi.

example: Hi, I have a reservation under the name Smith.
What time is check-out tomorrow?
Is breakfast included in the price?

pattern: I'd like to book a room.
example: I'd like to book a room for two nights.
exampleTr: İki gecelik bir oda rezerve etmek istiyorum.
pron: Ayd layk tu buk e rum.

example: Excuse me, where can I exchange money?
My flight has been delayed by two hours.

pattern: Could you call me a taxi?
example: Could you call me a taxi to the airport?
pron: Kud yu kol mi e teksi?

example: Is there free Wi-Fi in the hotel?

pattern: I'd like to extend my stay.
example: I'd like to extend my stay for one more night.
pron: Ayd layk tu ikstend may stey.

example: Excuse me, where is the boarding gate for flight 302?
exampleTr: Pardon, 302 sefer sayılı uçuşun biniş kapısı nerede?

example: I'd like a window seat, please.

pattern: How much is a one-way ticket?
example: How much is a one-way ticket to London?
exampleTr: Londra'ya tek yön bilet ne kadar?
pron: Hav maç iz e van-vey tikıt?

example: Is there a shuttle service to the airport?
Hello, I'd like to check in. My name is...

pattern: Can I have a wake-up call?
example: Can I have a wake-up call at 7 AM?
pron: Ken ay hev e veyk-ap kol?

example: Where is the luggage carousel for international flights?
exampleTr: Uluslararası uçuşlar için bagaj bandı nerede?

example: I've been waiting but my luggage is missing.
I don't feel well, I need to sit down.
Someone fainted, call an ambulance!
Where is the nearest hospital?

pattern: I need to see a doctor.
example: I've been coughing all week, I need to see a doctor.
exampleTr: Bütün hafta öksürüyorum, doktora görünmem lazım.
pron: Ay nid tu si e doktır.

example: Doctor, it hurts here when I press.
I have a terrible headache today.
exampleTr: Bugün korkunç bir baş ağrım var.

example: Can I get a prescription for this?
I have a fever and a sore throat.
I'm feeling dizzy, I need to sit down.
I twisted my ankle while running.
My back hurts, I need a painkiller.
exampleTr: Sırtım ağrıyor, ağrı kesiciye ihtiyacım var.

example: Is this illness contagious?
I'm having trouble breathing, please help.
exampleTr: Nefes almakta zorlanıyorum, lütfen yardım edin.

example: There's been an accident, please call for help!
exampleTr: Bir kaza oldu, lütfen yardım çağırın!

example: Are you free tomorrow evening?
I can't tonight, let's make it another time.
exampleTr: Bu gece olmaz, başka zamana erteleyelim.

example: I'm running out of time, hurry up!
The bus arrived just in time.

pattern: I'll be there in a minute.
example: Wait for me, I'll be there in a minute.
pron: Ayl bi der in e minıt.

example: You finally cleaned your room? It's about time!
exampleTr: Sonunda odanı mı temizledin? Artık zamanı gelmişti!

example: Sorry I'm late, I overslept.
exampleTr: Geç kaldığım için özür dilerim, uyuyakalmışım.

example: Is it already 6 PM? Time flies!
exampleTr: Şimdiden akşam 6 mı oldu? Zaman çok çabuk geçiyor!

example: We should meet, let's set a date.
Hold on, I'll be right back.
You finally started? Better late than never!
exampleTr: Sonunda mı başladın? Geç olsun güç olmasın!

example: Can we push the meeting back to 3 PM?
Good news, I'm ahead of schedule.
We need to hurry, we're behind schedule.
exampleTr: Acele etmemiz lazım, programa geride kaldık.

meaning: Zamanı unuttum / Vakit nasıl geçti anlamadım.
example: Sorry, I lost track of time.
It's getting late, I should go.
Dinner at 7? That would be great!
I'm afraid I can't make it tonight.
Good offer, I'll think about it.
Road trip this weekend? Count me in!
exampleTr: Bu hafta sonu yolculuk mu? Ben de varım!

example: I can't today, maybe next time.
exampleTr: Bugün olmaz, belki bir dahaki sefere.

example: Karaoke tonight? I'll pass.
exampleTr: Bu gece karaoke mi? Pas geçeyim.

example: Want to try sushi? Why not?
exampleTr: Suşi denemek ister misin? Neden olmasın?

example: Are you coming to the party? You bet!
Want to come over? I'd love to!
I wish I could, but I'm busy.
Do you agree? Absolutely!
You want me to jump? No way!
Pizza night? I'm down for that.
Meeting at 2? That works for me.

pattern: I'll have to take a rain check.
example: I'm swamped today, I'll have to take a rain check.
exampleTr: Bugün çok yoğunum, başka sefere artık.
pron: Ayl hev tu teyk e reyn çek.

example: Will you lend me your car? Not a chance!
Big decision, let me sleep on it.
Your exam is tomorrow? Break a leg!
The test was a piece of cake.
It's late, let's hit the road.
I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.
I eat fast food once in a blue moon.
exampleTr: Kırk yılda bir fast food yerim.

example: I know it's tough, but hang in there!
exampleTr: Zor olduğunu biliyorum ama pes etme!

example: I hate dentists but I need to bite the bullet.
exampleTr: Dişçilerden nefret ederim ama gözümü karartmam lazım.

example: I'm tired, let's call it a day.

pattern: The ball is in your court.
example: I made my offer, the ball is in your court.
pron: Dı bol iz in yor kort.

pattern: Don't judge a book by its cover.
example: He looks tough but he's kind. Don't judge a book by its cover.
exampleTr: Sert görünüyor ama iyi biri. Dış görünüşe göre yargılama.
pron: Dont cac e buk bay its kavır.

example: Stop promising and do it. Actions speak louder than words.
exampleTr: Söz vermeyi bırak ve yap. Laf değil icraat.

example: Take an umbrella, it's raining cats and dogs!

meaning: İyi saatte olsunlar! / Lafı geçen gelir.
example: We were just talking about you! Speak of the devil!

pattern: Kill two birds with one stone.
example: Let's shop and eat there, kill two birds with one stone.
exampleTr: Orada hem alışveriş yapalım hem yiyelim, bir taşla iki kuş.
pron: Kil tu bördz vit van ston.

example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.

pattern: Hit the nail on the head.
example: You hit the nail on the head with that comment.
pron: Hit dı neyl on dı hed.

example: You failed, but don't cry over spilt milk.

pattern: Every cloud has a silver lining.
example: You'll find a better job. Every cloud has a silver lining.
exampleTr: Daha iyi bir iş bulursun. Her şerde bir hayır vardır.
pron: Evri klaud hez e silvır layning.

example: I was burning the midnight oil to finish the project.
exampleTr: Projeyi bitirmek için gece yarılarına kadar çalıştım.

pattern: The early bird catches the worm.
example: Wake up early! The early bird catches the worm.
exampleTr: Erken kalk! Erken kalkan yol alır.
pron: Dı örli börd keçız dı vörm.

example: He'll clean his room? When pigs fly!

pattern: Cost an arm and a leg.
example: That car costs an arm and a leg.
exampleTr: O araba bir servete mal oluyor.
pron: Kost en arm end e leg.

example: The party got out of hand.

meaning: Ne düşünüyorsun? / Bir kuruşa düşüncelerini alayım.
example: You look quiet, a penny for your thoughts?

meaning: O kadar da zor değil / Uzay bilimi değil.
example: Just follow the instructions, it's not rocket science.
exampleTr: Talimatları takip et, o kadar da zor değil.

example: The plan failed, we're back to square one.
I can't wrap my head around this math problem.
Stop crying and pull yourself together.
I'm bored, let's hang out.
I'm throwing a party on Saturday, you should come!

pattern: Do you want to grab a coffee?
example: Do you want to grab a coffee after work?
exampleTr: İşten sonra kahve içmeye gidelim mi?
pron: Du yu vant tu greb e kofi?

example: The concert was amazing, I had a blast!
It's fair, let's split the bill.
Don't worry, dinner is on me tonight.
exampleTr: Merak etme, bu gece akşam yemeği benden.

example: Come in! Make yourself at home.
exampleTr: İçeri gel! Kendini evinde gibi hisset.

example: I'm not in the mood for going out.

pattern: That was a lot of fun!
example: Thanks for inviting me, that was a lot of fun!
exampleTr: Davet ettiğin için teşekkürler, çok eğlenceliydi!
pron: Det vaz e lat ov fan!

pattern: We should do this more often.
example: I missed this, we should do this more often.
pron: Vi şud du dis mor ofın.

example: I'll pick you up at 7.

pattern: What are you up to tonight?
example: Hey, what are you up to tonight?
pron: Vat ar yu ap tu tınayt?

example: Beach tomorrow? I'm game!
I'm getting tired, let's call it a night.
You paid last time, my treat today!
exampleTr: Geçen sefer sen ödedin, bugün benden!

example: Sorry, something came up, I can't make it.
exampleTr: Kusura bakma, bir şey çıktı, gelemiyorum.

example: Can I bring a plus one to the party?
You remembered my birthday? That's so thoughtful of you!
exampleTr: Doğum günümü hatırladın mı? Ne kadar düşüncelisin!

example: What's the weather like outside?
I can't go out, it's boiling hot.
Wear a jacket, it's freezing cold outside.

pattern: It looks like it's going to rain.
example: Take an umbrella, it looks like it's going to rain.
exampleTr: Şemsiye al, yağmur yağacak gibi görünüyor.
pron: İt luks layk its going tu reyn.

example: The sun is out, what a beautiful day!
exampleTr: Güneş çıkmış, ne güzel bir gün!

example: Pack layers, the weather is unpredictable.
exampleTr: Kat kat giy, hava tahmin edilemez.

example: Don't go out, it's pouring outside.
I forgot my umbrella, I'm soaking wet.

pattern: I need to do the laundry.
example: I'm out of clean clothes, I need to do the laundry.
exampleTr: Temiz kıyafetim kalmadı, çamaşır yıkamam lazım.
pron: Ay nid tu du dı londri.

example: I cooked, can you do the dishes?
exampleTr: Ben yemek yaptım, bulaşıkları yıkar mısın?

example: Guests are coming, let's tidy up.
Is dinner ready? I'm starving!
exampleTr: Akşam yemeği hazır mı? Açlıktan ölüyorum!

example: I'm hungry, what's for dinner?
Don't worry, I'll take out the trash.
Don't forget to lock the door when you leave.
Turn off the lights before you go to bed.
We're out of milk, I'll do the groceries.
Sit down, make yourself comfortable.
I've been working all day, I need a break.
exampleTr: Bütün gün çalıştım, biraz mola lazım.

example: I was so tired, I slept like a log.
I wake up at 6 AM, I'm a morning person.
I work best at night, I'm a night owl.
exampleTr: En iyi gece çalışırım, gece kuşuyum.

example: I can't connect, the Wi-Fi is down.
I'm running low on coffee.
What a long day, I'm beat.
I'm taking a course in web development.

pattern: I need to study for the exam.
example: Can't go out, I need to study for the exam.
pron: Ay nid tu stadi for dı igzem.

example: I'm struggling with grammar.
Sorry, can you explain that again?

pattern: I'm getting the hang of it.
example: It was hard at first, but I'm getting the hang of it.
pron: Aym geting dı heng ov it.

meaning: Hiçbir şey anlamadım / Kafamın üstünden geçti.
example: The lecture was so complex, it went over my head.
exampleTr: Ders çok karmaşıktı, hiçbir şey anlamadım.

example: Great news, I passed the exam!
I didn't study enough, I flunked the test.
Keep trying, practice makes perfect.
Don't worry, I'm a quick learner.
I missed the class, can I borrow your notes?
exampleTr: Dersi kaçırdım, notlarını ödünç alabilir miyim?

pattern: I learned it the hard way.
example: I learned it the hard way, always save your work.
exampleTr: Zor yoldan öğrendim, her zaman çalışmanı kaydet.
pron: Ay lörnd it dı hard vey.

example: I can't go out, I'm broke.
I'm saving up for a new laptop.
$50 for a t-shirt? That's a rip-off!
exampleTr: Bir tişört için 50 dolar mı? Bu soygun!

example: I'm on a tight budget this month.
Only $10? It's a bargain!
Can I pay in installments for this TV?

pattern: I need to cut back on spending.
example: I've been spending too much, I need to cut back.
pron: Ay nid tu kat bek on spending.

example: The quality is bad, it's not worth the price.
It's tough, I'm living paycheck to paycheck.
Be careful with money, it doesn't grow on trees.
I've been lazy, I need to work out.
I can't even run 5 minutes, I'm out of shape.
The weather is nice, let's go for a walk.
I pulled a muscle during the game.
Keep pushing, no pain, no gain!
exampleTr: Devam et, acı yoksa kazanç da yok!

example: I'm trying to be healthier, I'm cutting back on sugar.
exampleTr: Daha sağlıklı olmaya çalışıyorum, şekeri azaltıyorum.

example: I eat too much junk food, I should eat healthier.
exampleTr: Çok fazla abur cubur yiyorum, daha sağlıklı beslenmem lazım.

example: I feel energized, let's hit the gym!
I can't call, my phone died.
Wait, I'm charging my phone.
I can't load anything, the internet is slow.

pattern: Can you send me the link?
example: That looks cool, can you send me the link?
exampleTr: Güzel görünüyor, bana linki gönderir misin?
pron: Ken yu send mi dı link?

example: I'm not sure, let me Google it.
I lost all my work, my computer crashed.

pattern: I need to update my software.
example: It's not working, I need to update my software.
pron: Ay nid tu apdeyt may softvir.

pattern: Did you see what went viral?
example: Did you see what went viral on Twitter?
exampleTr: Twitter'da viral olan şeyi gördün mü?
pron: Did yu si vat vent vayrıl?

example: Let me DM you the details.
Can you take a screenshot and send it to me?
exampleTr: Ekran görüntüsü alıp bana gönderir misin?

example: I'm sorry about the mess.
Oh, my bad! I forgot to tell you.
exampleTr: Ah, benim hatam! Sana söylemeyi unuttum.

example: I apologize for the delay.
I'm sorry, it won't happen again.
You broke a glass? Don't worry about it.
It's okay, no hard feelings.
I know you didn't mean it, I forgive you.
We both made mistakes, let's put it behind us.
exampleTr: İkimiz de hata yaptık, bunu geride bırakalım.

example: I was wrong, I owe you an apology.
We argued, but that's water under the bridge now.
You want to start a business? Go for it!
exampleTr: İş mi kurmak istiyorsun? Hadi yap!

example: Don't be nervous, you've got this!
It's hard now but don't give up.
Great work, keep it up!
Don't stress too much, take it easy.
I don't know which job to pick. Follow your heart.
exampleTr: Hangi işi seçeceğimi bilmiyorum. Kalbinin sesini dinle.

example: Try bungee jumping, you only live once!
exampleTr: Bungee jumping dene, bir kere yaşarsın!

example: You didn't get the job, but look on the bright side.
exampleTr: İşi alamadın ama iyi tarafından bak.

example: Are you going to ask her? It's now or never!
exampleTr: Ona soracak mısın? Ya şimdi ya hiç!

example: The deadline is close, stay focused.
You can do it, believe in yourself!

pattern: Don't be so hard on yourself.
example: You tried your best, don't be so hard on yourself.
exampleTr: Elinden geleni yaptın, kendine bu kadar sert olma.
pron: Dont bi so hard on yorself.

example: We need creative solutions, think outside the box.
exampleTr: Yaratıcı çözümler lazım, kalıpların dışında düşün.

pattern: Where there's a will, there's a way.
example: Don't quit, where there's a will, there's a way.
pron: Ver derz e vil, derz e vey.

pattern: Step out of your comfort zone.
example: Try new things, step out of your comfort zone.
exampleTr: Yeni şeyler dene, konfor alanından çık.
pron: Step aut ov yor kamfırt zon.

example: You're on vacation, make the most of it!

pattern: I need to warm up first.
example: Wait, I need to warm up first before we start.
pron: Ay niid tu varm ap först.

example: It's a nice day, let's go for a run.
I work out every day to stay fit.
exampleTr: Formda kalmak için her gün spor yapıyorum.

example: I just turned on the TV. What's the score?
Did you see that? He scored a goal!
I've been hitting the gym three times a week.
exampleTr: Haftada üç kez spor salonuna gidiyorum.

example: Let's stretch before we start the hike.
I'm training for a marathon next month.

pattern: Do you want to play basketball?
example: Do you want to play basketball after work?
exampleTr: İşten sonra basketbol oynamak ister misin?
pron: Du yu vant tu pley baskit bol?

example: Great workout! I need to cool down now.
exampleTr: Harika antrenman! Şimdi soğuma yapmam lazım.

example: Don't go out, it's freezing outside.
The sun is shining, let's go to the park.
Stay home, we're expecting a storm tonight.
exampleTr: Evde kal, bu gece fırtına bekleniyor.

example: Don't go out now, it's pouring!
Let's go inside, the wind is picking up.
It's so humid today, I can barely breathe.
The flowers are blooming, spring is in the air.
I love autumn leaves, they're so colorful.
Take a jacket, it's a bit chilly outside.

pattern: We had a heatwave last week.
example: We had a heatwave last week, it was unbearable.
exampleTr: Geçen hafta sıcak dalgası vardı, dayanılmazdı.
pron: Vi hed e hiitgveyv last viik.

example: That was my bad, I should have checked first.

pattern: I hope you can forgive me.
example: I'm truly sorry, I hope you can forgive me.
pron: Ay hop yu ken forgiv mi.

pattern: I won't let it happen again.
example: I'm sorry I was late. I won't let it happen again.
exampleTr: Geç kaldığım için özür dilerim. Bir daha olmayacak.
pron: Ay vont let it hepın egeyn.

example: I feel terrible about missing your birthday.
exampleTr: Doğum gününü kaçırdığım için çok kötü hissediyorum.

example: You said sorry? Apology accepted.
exampleTr: Özür mü diledin? Özür kabul edildi.

example: Twenty dollars for a coffee? It's a rip-off!
exampleTr: Bir kahveye yirmi dolar mı? Kazıklama bu!

example: I need to save up for a new laptop.
exampleTr: Yeni bir laptop için biriktirmem lazım.

example: Only five dollars? That's a bargain!
exampleTr: Sadece beş dolar mı? Bu çok ucuz!

example: I paid too much, I got ripped off.
I need to restart it, my phone is acting up.
exampleTr: Yeniden başlatmam lazım, telefonum sorun çıkarıyor.

example: I lost my work because the app crashed.
I can't take photos, I ran out of storage.

pattern: I need to charge my phone.
example: My battery is at 5%. I need to charge my phone.
exampleTr: Şarjım yüzde beşte. Telefonumu şarj etmem lazım.
pron: Ay niid tu çarc may fon.

example: I can't log in, the website is down.
I'm not sure, let me Google it.
I can't stream, my internet is laggy.
Don't worry, I'm a fast learner.

pattern: I need to brush up on my English.
example: I have an interview, I need to brush up on my English.
pron: Ay niid tu braş ap on may İngliş.

pattern: Can you explain it in simple terms?
example: I didn't get it. Can you explain it in simple terms?
pron: Ken yu ekspleyn it in simpıl törmz?

example: We can't hear you, you're on mute.
I moved to a better spot. Can you hear me now?
exampleTr: Daha iyi bir yere geçtim. Şimdi duyabiliyor musun?

example: I'll text you the details later tonight.
I can't load anything, my signal is weak.
This is too complicated to text. Let's jump on a call.
exampleTr: Yazarak çok karışık. Hadi bir arayalım.

example: I can't focus, I have a splitting headache.
Be careful, I'm allergic to peanuts.
I'm not coming to work, I'm feeling under the weather.
I heard you're sick. Get well soon!
I'm not busy, I'm just killing time.
I'm fed up with this traffic.
Free vacation? What's the catch?
exampleTr: Bedava tatil mi? İşin puanı ne?

example: Free money? That's a no-brainer!
exampleTr: Bedava para mı? Çok kolay bir karar!

example: Great presentation, you nailed it!
Just tell me, don't beat around the bush.
I like both options, I'm sitting on the fence.
You can't change it now, that ship has sailed.
exampleTr: Artık değiştiremezsin, o iş çoktan geçti.

pattern: I'll keep you in the loop.
example: I'll keep you in the loop about the project.
pron: Ayl kiip yu in dı luup.

example: I can't make it, I'm swamped with work.

pattern: We need to meet the deadline.
example: Hurry up, we need to meet the deadline.
exampleTr: Acele et, son teslim tarihine yetişmemiz lazım.
pron: Vi niid tu miit dı dedlayn.

pattern: I'll get back to you on that.
example: Good question. I'll get back to you on that.
exampleTr: İyi soru. O konuda sana döneceğim.
pron: Ayl get bek tu yu on det.

example: We've been here for hours. Let's wrap this up.
I'm exhausted. I need a day off.
exampleTr: Bitkinim. Bir gün izne ihtiyacım var.

example: I got the job! I'm over the moon!
That noise drives me crazy.
I'm torn between the two options.
The ending of that movie blew my mind.
exampleTr: O filmin sonu aklımı başımdan aldı.

example: You're right, I couldn't agree more.
Don't argue with him, it's not worth it.

pattern: I'm on the fence about it.
example: I'm on the fence about buying a new car.
pron: Aym on dı fens ebaunt it.

example: I need cash. Where's the nearest ATM?
exampleTr: Nakit lazım. En yakın ATM nerede?
pron: Verz dı niirest ey tii em?

pattern: The room has a nice view.
example: Come look, the room has a nice view of the sea.
exampleTr: Gel bak, odanın deniz manzarası çok güzel.
pron: Dı ruum hez e nays vyuu.

example: I just landed and I lost my luggage.

pattern: Can you recommend a good restaurant?
example: We're new here. Can you recommend a good restaurant?
exampleTr: Burada yeniyiz. İyi bir restoran önerebilir misiniz?
pron: Ken yu rekomend e gud restorant?

pattern: Would you mind closing the door?
example: It's cold. Would you mind closing the door?
pron: Vud yu maynd klozing dı dor?

example: This is hard. I'd appreciate your help.

pattern: Can you do me a favor?
example: Hey, can you do me a favor?
exampleTr: Hey, bana bir iyilik yapar mısın?
pron: Ken yu du mi e feyvır?

pattern: Is it okay if I sit here?
example: Excuse me, is it okay if I sit here?
pron: İz it okey if ay sit hiir?

pattern: I hate to bother you, but...
example: I hate to bother you, but can I ask a question?
exampleTr: Rahatsız etmek istemem ama bir soru sorabilir miyim?
pron: Ay heyt tu badır yu bat...

example: Me and Tom go way back, since high school.
exampleTr: Ben ve Tom çok eskiye dayanırız, liseden beri.

example: I missed you. Let's catch up over coffee.
exampleTr: Seni özledim. Kahve içip sohbet edelim.

example: Everyone likes him, he's a people person.

pattern: We hit it off right away.
example: We met at a party and hit it off right away.
exampleTr: Bir partide tanıştık ve hemen kaynaştık.
pron: Vi hit it of rayt evey.

pattern: Do you have this in a larger size?
meaning: Bunun daha büyük bedeni var mı?
example: This is too small. Do you have this in a larger size?
exampleTr: Bu çok küçük. Bunun daha büyük bedeni var mı?
pron: Du yu hev dis in e larcır sayz?

example: Compliments to the chef, the food was amazing.
Let's be fair. Can we split the bill?
We need to go shopping, the fridge is empty.
I left my keys inside, I locked myself out.
exampleTr: Anahtarlarımı içeride unuttum, kapıyı üzerime kilitledim.

example: Thanks for the offer, but I'll pass on that.
exampleTr: Teklif için teşekkürler ama ben pas geçeyim.

example: Hiking in the rain? Count me out.
Don't wait for me, I'm running behind.

pattern: Are we going the right way?
example: I don't recognize this area. Are we going the right way?
exampleTr: Bu bölgeyi tanımıyorum. Doğru yoldan mı gidiyoruz?
pron: Ar vi going dı rayt vey?

example: I have to go now. It was nice meeting you.
exampleTr: Şimdi gitmem lazım. Tanıştığıma memnun oldum.

pattern: Say hi to your family for me.
example: I'm leaving now. Say hi to your family for me.
exampleTr: Ben gidiyorum. Ailene benden selam söyle.
pron: Sey hay tu yor femili for mi.

pattern: I'm not in the mood for that.
example: Let's do something else, I'm not in the mood for that.
exampleTr: Başka bir şey yapalım, onun için modumda değilim.
pron: Aym nat in dı muud for det.

example: Work is so stressful. - Tell me about it!
exampleTr: İş çok stresli. - Bana mı anlatıyorsun!

example: Just be confident! - Easier said than done.
I have a fever, I'm calling in sick today.
exampleTr: Ateşim var, bugün hastalık izni alıyorum.

example: I fell down the stairs and sprained my ankle.
exampleTr: Merdivenlerden düştüm ve ayak bileğimi burktum.

example: I need medicine. Where's the nearest pharmacy?
exampleTr: İlaca ihtiyacım var. En yakın eczane nerede?

example: Sorry I'm late, I overslept this morning.
exampleTr: Geç kaldığım için özür dilerim, bu sabah uyuyakaldım.

pattern: I'm afraid I can't make it.
example: I'm afraid I can't make it to the party.
pron: Aym efreyd ay kent meyk it.

example: Dinner at 7? Sounds like a plan!
exampleTr: Saat 7'de akşam yemeği mi? Kulağa iyi geliyor!

pattern: I'll be there in a jiffy.
example: Wait for me, I'll be there in a jiffy.
pron: Ayl bi der in e cifi.

example: It's hard but don't give up!
Great results, keep up the good work!

pattern: How far is it from here?
example: How far is it from here to the airport?
pron: Hau far iz it from hiir?

example: Go straight and take the second left.
exampleTr: Düz git ve ikinci soldan dön.

example: I didn't expect to see you here! What a pleasant surprise!
exampleTr: Seni burada görmeyi beklemiyordum! Ne güzel sürpriz!

example: I'm moving away, but keep in touch!

pattern: It's on the tip of my tongue.
example: I know the answer, it's on the tip of my tongue.
pron: İts on dı tip ov may tang.

pattern: I have mixed feelings about it.
example: I have mixed feelings about the new plan.
exampleTr: Yeni plan hakkında karışık duygularım var.
pron: Ay hev mikst fiilings ebaunt it.

example: Don't ask too many questions, curiosity killed the cat.
exampleTr: Çok fazla soru sorma, merak insanı mahveder.

example: Let's go Dutch this time, it's only fair.
exampleTr: Bu sefer hesabı ayrı ayrı ödeyelim, adil olan bu.

pattern: I'll send you a voice note.
example: It's too long to type, I'll send you a voice note.
exampleTr: Yazmak çok uzun, sana sesli mesaj atarım.
pron: Ayl send yu e voys not.

pattern: Rome wasn't built in a day.
example: Be patient, Rome wasn't built in a day.
exampleTr: Sabırlı ol, Roma bir günde kurulmadı.
pron: Rom vazınt bilt in e dey.

example: My room is a mess, I need to tidy up.
Sorry we're late, we're stuck in traffic.
exampleTr: Geç kaldığımız için üzgünüz, trafiğe takıldık.
